finding.summary: Limited spectral data prevents conclusive diagnosis. The EQ applies +3 dB upper mid boost, which may contribute to the moderately high crest factor (12 dB) and integrated LUFS of -20, but no frequency content below 3150 Hz is available to assess low-end balance or potential muddiness.
finding.probable_cause: Insufficient spectral information from measurement (only two high-frequency bands provided).
finding.confidence_reason: Only two 1/3-octave bands (3150 Hz and 5000 Hz) are present, none below 3 kHz; critical low-mid and bass information is missing. The isolated capture is verified, but the data is too sparse for a reliable tonal diagnosis.

evidence_refs.description: Upper Mid Gain set to 0.64 normalized (+3 dB)

proposal.reason: Insufficient evidence to recommend a safe, evidence-based adjustment. The only adjustable parameter is the Upper Mid Gain, but its effect on the overall tone cannot be evaluated without broader spectral data. No change proposed.
